name: html-structure-validate
description: Validate HTML5 structure and basic syntax. BLOCKING quality gate - stops pipeline if validation fails. Ensures deterministic output quality.
capabilities: Checks proper tag closure and nesting
Verifies required meta tags and title
author: AbeJitsu
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only SKILL.md and skill-report.json. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or file operations are present. The skill describes how to validate HTML structure using an external Python tool. Static scanner detected documentation patterns (code examples, file paths, hash values) that triggered false positive security rules.
valueStatement: AI-generated HTML often contains structural errors that cause rendering issues. This quality gate validates HTML5 compliance, tag closure, and required elements before the pipeline continues.
limitations: Does not validate CSS or JavaScript syntax
Does not fix HTML errors automatically

useCases.title: HTML Quality Gate
useCases.description: Ensures AI-generated book pages meet HTML structural standards before publishing

useCases.title: Pipeline Validation
useCases.description: Validates generated HTML files as part of automated content processing pipelines

useCases.title: Structural Compliance
useCases.description: Checks that all pages contain required elements and proper HTML structure

promptTemplates.title: Basic Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the html-structure-validate skill to check 04_page_XX.html for HTML5 compliance and proper structure. Report any errors.

promptTemplates.title: Strict Mode
promptTemplates.prompt: Run html-structure-validate in strict mode on chapter_XX page HTML. Include warnings in the failure criteria.

promptTemplates.title: Batch Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use html-structure-validate to check all generated pages in chapter_XX. Generate individual validation reports for each page.

promptTemplates.title: CI Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Run html-structure-validate as a quality gate. Fail the pipeline if any page fails structure validation. Output results to validation_reports/.

bestPractices: Run validation as the first quality gate before semantic analysis
Configure strict mode for production content pipelines
Review validation reports to identify patterns in AI-generated HTML issues
antiPatterns: Skipping validation and proceeding with malformed HTML
Ignoring warnings that accumulate across multiple pages
Using validation as a replacement for prompt engineering

faq.question: Does this skill fix HTML errors?
faq.answer: No. This skill validates and reports errors only. You must fix issues manually or regenerate with improved prompts.

faq.question: What HTML elements are required?
faq.answer: Required: DOCTYPE, html, head, body, title, CSS link, page-container div, and main content area.

faq.question: Can I skip validation?
faq.answer: Yes, but this is a blocking quality gate. Skipping may result in broken or non-compliant HTML in your output.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: This skill reads specified HTML files and generates local JSON reports. No data leaves your machine.

faq.question: Why does validation fail sometimes?
faq.answer: Common causes: missing DOCTYPE, unclosed tags, missing title tag, no CSS link, or empty content areas.

faq.question: How is this different from semantic validation?
faq.answer: Structure validation checks HTML syntax and required elements. Semantic validation checks meaning and context.
